# UC-22.8.27 · SOC 2 A1 — Capacity Planning Signals for In-Scope Production Services

## Description

A1 addresses availability commitments. Forecasting CPU, memory, queue depth, or connection pool utilization against committed headroom demonstrates proactive capacity management—not reactive firefighting after outages.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes metrics. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Set threshold to contractual headroom; (2) join with change calendar for planned traffic spikes; (3) feed capacity committee slide deck; (4) document assumptions in system description; (5) alert infra 30 days before sustained breach predicted. Indexes required: `index=metrics`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Observability Cloud / Infrastructure monitoring, or `metrics` index.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=metrics` `metric_name IN ("cpu.util","mem.util","queue.depth")` `entity=prod_payments_*` earliest=-90d.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Set threshold to contractual headroom; (2) join with change calendar for planned traffic spikes; (3) feed capacity committee slide deck; (4) document assumptions in system description; (5) alert infra 30 days before sustained breach predicted.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=metrics metric_name="cpu.util" entity=prod_payments_* earliest=-90d
| timechart span=1d avg(_value) as avg_cpu
| predict avg_cpu as forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=14 period=7
| where forecast>85
| table _time, avg_cpu, forecast
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 A1 — Capacity Planning Signals for In-Scope Production Services** — A1 addresses availability commitments. Forecasting CPU, memory, queue depth, or connection pool utilization against committed headroom demonstrates proactive capacity management—not reactive firefighting after outages.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=metrics` `metric_name IN ("cpu.util","mem.util","queue.depth")` `entity=prod_payments_*` earliest=-90d. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Observability Cloud / Infrastructure monitoring, or `metrics` index. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: metrics.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=metrics, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

• Filters the current rows with `where forecast>85` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (avg_cpu + forecast), Single value (days until threshold), Table.

## SPL

```spl
index=metrics metric_name="cpu.util" entity=prod_payments_* earliest=-90d
| timechart span=1d avg(_value) as avg_cpu
| predict avg_cpu as forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=14 period=7
| where forecast>85
| table _time, avg_cpu, forecast
```

## Visualization

Line chart (avg_cpu + forecast), Single value (days until threshold), Table.
